channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Graffiti on front elevation of 112 Anerley Road, 9th March
item.description:
Non-offensive graffiti of pink and white spray on black plywood hoarding on front elevation of 112 Anerley Road. Please see to photos attached. Covering an area of approx 3.5m across and 1.5m tall.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 112, Anerley Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Council estate door is broken, 9th March
item.description:
Door does not shut and emergency opening is broken

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Evering Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Grafiti on gable end of 124 Anerley Road, 9th March
item.description:
Non-offensive graffiti of black spray tag on white painted brick wall, on gable end of 124 Anerley Road. Please see to photo attached. Approx 75cm across and 50cm tall

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 124, Anerley Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped rubbish from public, 9th March
item.description:
Councillor report. Been here 2 / 3 weeks. Starting to smell and causing issues. Please collect asap

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 70, Anerley Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: On pavement, outside 17 Versailles Road, rubble dumped, 9th March
item.description:
On pavement, outside 17 Versailles Road, rubble dumped

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 17, Versailles Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Constant fly tipping on my drive., 9th March
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to request assistance regarding ongoing issues we have been experiencing at our property during our building works. Over the past few months, we have had several incidents of fly tipping on our driveway. On each occasion, I have had to arrange and pay for the removal of the waste myself. Unfortunately, the situation has continued and I have now reached the point where I feel I need the council’s support. Currently, there are two fridges that have been dumped on our drive and tipped over. Last month, a bed and mattress were also fly tipped outside our property. The mattress alone cost me a considerable amount of money to have removed. In addition to this, we are also experiencing ongoing issues with vehicles parking across our driveway, preventing access. This has caused further problems with the skip company collecting our skip during the building works, as they charge a call-out fee each time they are unable to access it. These issues are becoming increasingly difficult and costly for us to manage ourselves. I would therefore appreciate it if the council could investigate this matter and advise what action can be taken to help prevent further fly tipping and parking obstruction outside our property. Thank you for your time and assistance. I look forward to hearing from you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20, Hamlet Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 26 Mapesbury Road NW2, 9th March
item.description:
Road in very bad condition

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mapesbury Road, London

item.title: White spray paint on wall, 9th March
item.description:
Non-offensive graffiti of white spray on brown brick wall, approx 50-75cm wide, by 50cm tall. On boundary wall of Brooklyn Houase, on junction of Anerley Road with Versailles Road. Please see photo attached

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Versailles Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Excessive Public Urination, Littering, Fly Tipping, Crack pipes, 9th March
item.description:
I live at 336 Mare Street and there is a massive issue of littering right at pur doorstep, also the corner right next to our front door (red door) has become an extremely popular public urination spot. It reeks everyday so strongly of urine its extreme.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mare Street, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped Rubbish in Street, 9th March
item.description:
There is rubbish being repeatedly left outside door 23-25 Homerton High Street. This ends up being kicked about, presumably torn open by foxes/cats and is then scattered about the street. The City Academy school is opposite, it is disgusting for all.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Furrow Lane, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped in black open sacks, 9th March
item.description:
Black sacks dumped bags are open and spilling onto pavement food and nappy waste. Attracting vermin.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brookdene Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rear garden of 387F Finchley Road, 9th March
item.description:
A large branch fell off over the weekend, no strong winds, it may be a diseased tree, best to inspect it in case.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Finchley Road, West Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown ivy at 84, 9th March

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 86, Talfourd Road, Peckham, Greater London

item.title: Graffiti on street cabinet oposite 83 Anerley Road, 9th March

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Anerley Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London

item.title: Sacks of rubbish dumped, 9th March
item.description:
Bags have ‘commercial waste, City of Westminster’ printed on them. Long way to come to dump waste. This is beginning to be a daily dump spot. Thanks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Church Manorway, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet
